name: Connee Boswell
abstract: Constance Foore "Connee" Boswell (December 3, 1907 – October 11, 1976) was an American vocalist born in Kansas City, Missouri, but raised in New Orleans, Louisiana. With sisters Martha and Helvetia "Vet", she performed in the 1920s and 1930s as the trio The Boswell Sisters.

about_quotes.speaker: Jay Cee Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Some of J. C.'s songs were recorded by the following people: ... Connie Boswell ...

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer lists Connie Boswell as a musician featured in the film 'Syncopation' alongside Charlie Barnet; the interviewee confirms they recorded the jam session sequence with 'Gene and all the people you mentioned' but does not describe a personal or specific professional relationship with Con

about_quotes.speaker: Buzzy Williams
about_quotes.evidence: We had Sophie Tuggett, Joey Lewis, and Connie Boswell... I was with him, oh I made some money with that guy.

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: we were in with them where we had Connie Boswell, Jack Haley. We had George Jessel...

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Humes
about_quotes.evidence: You said when you were with Connie that you played at Streets of Paris.
